key_insight: Three commercial multimodal LLMs showed vendor-specific but clinically unacceptable errors on breast pathology tasks, especially subtype and HER2 assessment.
clinical_relevance: These models are not ready for autonomous diagnosis or treatment-determining breast pathology, but may be useful only as supervised drafting aids.
method: 427 invasive breast cancer cases were evaluated with identical H&E image tiles and prompts across Claude Sonnet 4.6, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and GPT-5.5.
Performance was compared against institutional pathology reports using concordance, sensitivity, specificity, Cohen’s kappa, and pairwise statistical tests.
Twelve prompt variants were tested across 4,056 total calls to probe whether prompt changes improved accuracy.
evidence_signal: In the 427-case evaluation, the models failed in different fixed directions and did not reliably identify HER2-positive disease, with prompt variation not restoring sensitivity.
watch_item: Performance may shift with vendor, prompt, or case mix, but changing systems appears to change the error pattern rather than solve the underlying reliability problem.
paper_title: Three multimodal large language models fail at clinically actionable breast pathology in three different directions
